frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: persistent neural activity tracks evolving sensory evidence toward a decision

frameworks.name: intentional / motor-preparatory account of decision making
frameworks.one_line: decision variables are carried by activity tied to prospective actions

frameworks.name: sensory categorical representation in LIP
frameworks.one_line: stimulus-driven categorical responses as an alternative locus for the decision variable

core_question: Do LIP sensory responses, like motor-preparatory responses, also track the evolving evidence during perceptual decision making?
key_assumptions: LIP neurons have distinguishable sensory and oculomotor-preparatory response components
direction discrimination of random-dot motion engages evidence accumulation
placing motion stimulus vs choice target in the response field isolates sensory vs motor signals
manipulations: whether the random-dot motion patch was in the neuron's response field
whether the saccade choice target was in the neuron's response field
key_findings: both stimulus-in-RF and target-in-RF configurations produced task-related LIP activity
only motor-preparatory responses reflected evidence accumulation
sensory responses did not track the evolving decision variable
interpretations: evolving decision processes are supported by persistent activity tied to actions/intentions rather than to higher-order stimulus representations
